title: Last Night on Earth: The Zombie Game - a look at the web-exclusive expansions
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uk5ADKNqHoc

A short addendum to his solo deep-dive, Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through the five web-exclusive expansions for **Last Night on Earth**, ordered from Flying Frog directly (about $10 each, ~$60 all in) and — living close by — delivered almost overnight. No retail packaging, which he actually prefers.

The headline pack is **Advanced Abilities**, which introduces the experience system and gives most heroes a unique upgrade card to buy with XP, plus four zombie advanced abilities (swarm, ravenous, outbreak, infectious) and a few new balanced cards for each deck. His one disappointment: the hero cards don't cover the Hero Pack 2 characters, so some heroes are left without an advanced ability.

The other four are scenarios, each bundled with a small deck of new hero and zombie cards: **Stock Up** (gather weapons and a first-aid kit into a safe building), **Hold the Line** (a tower-defense stand where zombies march from a board edge), **Revenge of the Dead** (an accelerated, extra-deadly last stand), and **Air Strike** (light flares to signal survivors before the military levels the town). His pick of the litter: Revenge of the Dead. For a solo player craving variety, he judges the set worth it.

----

These expansions add variety but ask $60 for cards and rules sheets with no packaging. For a game you already love solo, is that an easy yes or a hard sell?
